summary: Security scan completed. See the findings table below for details.

findings.title: DOM-based XSS via unsanitized userName in innerHTML
findings.description: User input 'userName' is directly injected into innerHTML, enabling DOM-based XSS attacks.
findings.recommendation: Sanitize or encode userName before insertion into innerHTML, or use safer DOM manipulation methods (e.g., textContent).

findings.title: DOM-based XSS via document.write of unsanitized query
findings.description: User-controlled 'query' is written directly via document.write, allowing injection of malicious scripts.
findings.recommendation: Avoid using document.write with untrusted input. Use safe DOM methods with encoding or sanitization.

findings.title: DOM-based XSS via unsanitized location.hash insertion into innerHTML
findings.description: Data from location.hash is directly inserted into innerHTML without sanitization, enabling XSS via URL fragments.
findings.recommendation: Sanitize or encode the 'name' variable before insertion or use textContent.

findings.title: Reflected XSS via direct injection of query parameter into HTML
findings.description: The 'q' query parameter is directly included in response HTML without encoding, enabling reflected XSS.
findings.recommendation: Properly encode user input before embedding in HTML output.

findings.title: Reflected XSS via direct injection of URL param into HTML
findings.description: URL parameter 'name' is embedded directly into HTML without sanitization or encoding, causing reflected XSS.
findings.recommendation: Escape or encode output when rendering user-supplied data.

findings.title: Stored XSS via unescaped storage and rendering of comment
findings.description: User comments are stored and later injected into innerHTML without sanitization, enabling stored XSS.
findings.recommendation: Sanitize or encode stored content before rendering. Use safe DOM manipulation methods.

findings.title: Insecure use of eval() on user input
findings.description: Evaluating user input with eval() enables arbitrary code execution.
findings.recommendation: Avoid eval() on untrusted input. Use safer alternatives or restricted expression parsers.

findings.title: Insecure use of Function constructor on user input
findings.description: Using Function constructor on untrusted config string can lead to arbitrary code execution.
findings.recommendation: Avoid dynamic code execution on untrusted input; use safer parsing methods.

findings.title: Open Redirect via unvalidated query parameter
findings.description: The 'url' query parameter is directly used in redirect without validation, exposing phishing risks.
findings.recommendation: Validate redirect targets against an allowlist or use relative paths only.

findings.title: CSRF vulnerability due to missing CSRF token validation
findings.description: No CSRF protection exists for money transfer endpoint; attacker sites can trigger transfers.
findings.recommendation: Implement CSRF tokens or same-site cookies to protect state-changing operations.
